benchmark_metadata.purpose: Deep-dive tongue & neck cancer benchmark — 30 cases covering diverse subsites, stages, molecular profiles, and clinical scenarios

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue squamous cell carcinoma — early stage
cases.why_this_case_matters: Early-stage tongue SCC in a young non-smoker — unusual presentation. Tests whether strategy captures surgery-alone approach, sentinel lymph node biopsy debate, and avoids overtreatment.

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue squamous cell carcinoma — locally advanced with mandible invasion
cases.why_this_case_matters: Locally advanced tongue SCC requiring composite resection (mandibulectomy + free flap). Tests reconstructive considerations, adjuvant CRT decisions, and multi-mutation targeted options.

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue squamous cell carcinoma with perineural invasion
cases.stage: Stage II (T2N0M0, depth of invasion >5mm, PNI+)
cases.why_this_case_matters: PNI is a high-risk pathologic feature that upgrades adjuvant therapy. Tests whether strategy correctly identifies PNI as indication for adjuvant RT and captures the depth-of-invasion >4mm neck dissection threshold.

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue squamous cell carcinoma — recurrent after primary surgery and radiation

risk_factors: prior tongue SCC treated 18 months ago

cases.why_this_case_matters: Recurrence in irradiated field — reirradiation vs salvage surgery debate. Tests strategy handling of previously treated patients, cumulative toxicity, and immunotherapy as salvage option.

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue squamous cell carcinoma — metastatic with lung metastases
cases.why_this_case_matters: Metastatic oral tongue SCC — palliative intent. Tests KEYNOTE-048 paradigm, HRAS mutation targeting (tipifarnib), and transition from curative to palliative goals. CPS>=50 favors pembrolizumab monotherapy.

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue verrucous carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Verrucous carcinoma is a distinct low-grade variant — radiation may cause anaplastic transformation. Tests whether strategy correctly avoids RT as primary and recommends wide local excision.

cases.cancer_type: Base of tongue squamous cell carcinoma — HPV-positive
cases.why_this_case_matters: HPV+ base of tongue is the most common HPV-associated oropharyngeal cancer. Different from oral tongue (oropharyngeal vs oral cavity). Tests de-escalation approaches and TORS for base of tongue.

cases.cancer_type: Base of tongue squamous cell carcinoma — HPV-negative, locally advanced
cases.why_this_case_matters: HPV-negative base of tongue with poor performance status and liver disease. Tests cisplatin eligibility assessment, nutrition optimization, cetuximab as alternative, and palliative vs curative intent decision-making.

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue squamous cell carcinoma — young non-smoker with genetic predisposition
cases.why_this_case_matters: Genetic predisposition cancers have altered treatment sensitivity — Fanconi anemia increases chemo/RT toxicity. Tests whether strategy captures DNA repair-deficient tumor biology and modified treatment protocols.

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue mucoepidermoid carcinoma (minor salivary gland origin)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Minor salivary gland tumor of the tongue — different biology from SCC. Tests strategy handling of non-SCC tongue malignancies, HER2 testing/targeting, and the distinction between oral cavity SCC and salivary protocols.

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue malignant melanoma (mucosal melanoma)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Mucosal melanoma of the tongue is extremely rare and aggressive. Tests strategy's ability to pivot from H&N SCC protocols to melanoma-specific approaches (anti-PD-1, KIT inhibitors, nivo+ipi).

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue squamous cell carcinoma with synchronous second primary (floor of mouth)
cases.stage: Stage III (tongue T2N1M0 + floor of mouth T1N0M0)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Synchronous second primary — field cancerization from tobacco/alcohol. Tests composite resection planning, wide-field RT considerations, and chemoprevention for third primary prevention.

cases.cancer_type: Tongue base adenoid cystic carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Adenoid cystic carcinoma has unique biology — slow-growing but relentless with perineural spread. Tests whether strategy captures ACC-specific approaches (lenvatinib, axitinib, MYB-targeting, and neutron/proton therapy).

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue squamous cell carcinoma — superficial spreading, extensive
cases.stage: Stage II (T2N0M0, superficial pattern, 8cm surface area)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Immunosuppressed patient with extensive superficial disease. Tests photodynamic therapy, the challenge of wide excision with functional preservation, and modified immunotherapy approach in autoimmune disease context.

cases.cancer_type: Oral tongue squamous cell carcinoma — post-transplant (immunosuppression-related)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Post-transplant malignancy — immunotherapy risks graft rejection. Tests strategy's handling of the immunotherapy-transplant dilemma and modified surgical approach. PD-L1 high but checkpoint inhibitors could destroy the kidney graft.

cases.cancer_type: Cervical lymph node SCC — unknown primary, HPV-positive
cases.why_this_case_matters: Unknown primary with HPV+ neck node — likely occult oropharyngeal primary. Tests diagnostic workup (PET, tonsillectomy, tongue base mucosectomy), treatment of the unknown primary, and whether to irradiate pharyngeal axis.

cases.cancer_type: Cervical lymph node SCC — unknown primary, HPV-negative
cases.why_this_case_matters: HPV-negative unknown primary has worse prognosis. Tests comprehensive mucosal irradiation vs focused treatment, neck dissection role, and immunotherapy integration.

cases.cancer_type: Medullary thyroid carcinoma — sporadic, RET M918T mutated
cases.why_this_case_matters: Medullary thyroid carcinoma — distinct from differentiated thyroid cancer (no RAI). Tests RET-targeted therapy (selpercatinib/pralsetinib), vandetanib/cabozantinib as older options, and calcitonin monitoring.

cases.cancer_type: Follicular thyroid carcinoma — RAI-refractory, progressive
cases.stage: Stage II (T3N0M1, bone metastases, AJCC 8th age <55)
cases.why_this_case_matters: RAI-refractory follicular thyroid cancer with bone mets. Tests lenvatinib/sorafenib (SELECT/DECISION trials), redifferentiation strategies (MEK inhibitors to restore RAI uptake), bone-directed therapy, and NRAS-targeted approaches.

cases.cancer_type: Hurthle cell carcinoma of thyroid — widely invasive
cases.why_this_case_matters: Hurthle cell carcinoma is a distinct thyroid cancer with variable RAI avidity and unique mitochondrial biology. Tests whether strategy correctly identifies it as separate from follicular, addresses RAI trial, and includes lenvatinib/sorafenib.

cases.cancer_type: Supraglottic laryngeal squamous cell carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Supraglottic cancer differs from glottic — higher nodal metastasis rate, different surgical approaches (supraglottic laryngectomy). Tests organ preservation with concurrent CRT vs surgery, aspiration risk management.

cases.cancer_type: Subglottic squamous cell carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Subglottic SCC is the rarest laryngeal subsite with worst prognosis. Often presents with airway compromise. Tests emergency airway management, total laryngectomy vs CRT, and paratracheal nodal coverage.

cases.cancer_type: Primary tracheal squamous cell carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Primary tracheal cancer is extremely rare (~0.1/100k). Tests strategy handling of ultra-rare cancers with limited trial data. Tracheal resection, bronchoscopic interventions, and extrapolation from lung/laryngeal SCC data.

cases.cancer_type: Cervical esophageal squamous cell carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Cervical esophageal SCC is treated more like H&N cancer than thoracic esophageal. Tests definitive CRT (RTOG 85-01) vs pharyngo-laryngo-esophagectomy, nivolumab (CheckMate-577 adjuvant), and nutritional support.

cases.cancer_type: Carotid body paraganglioma (neck paraganglioma)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Non-epithelial neck tumor — completely different biology. Tests strategy's handling of paragangliomas (surgery vs observation vs RT), SDHB mutation surveillance for metachronous tumors, and genetic counseling.

cases.cancer_type: Neck soft tissue sarcoma — synovial sarcoma of the neck
cases.why_this_case_matters: Sarcoma of the neck — radically different from carcinoma. Tests strategy pivot to sarcoma protocols (wide excision, doxorubicin/ifosfamide, pazopanib), NY-ESO-1 vaccine, and the challenge of achieving clear margins in the neck.

cases.cancer_type: Poorly differentiated thyroid carcinoma (PDTC)
cases.why_this_case_matters: PDTC sits between differentiated and anaplastic thyroid cancer — intermediate prognosis. Tests whether strategy correctly distinguishes PDTC management, partial RAI responsiveness, and role of external beam RT + systemic therapy.

cases.cancer_type: Parapharyngeal space pleomorphic adenoma — malignant transformation (carcinoma ex pleomorphic adenoma)

risk_factors: long-standing parotid mass (20 years, presumed benign)

cases.why_this_case_matters: Carcinoma ex pleomorphic adenoma is a rare malignant transformation of a benign tumor. Tests whether strategy captures the unique surgical challenges of parapharyngeal space, extent of malignant component assessment, and limited evidence base.

cases.cancer_type: Cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma of the neck — high-risk with in-transit metastases
cases.why_this_case_matters: Cutaneous SCC of neck in transplant patient — cemiplimab/pembrolizumab approved for cSCC but risk graft rejection. Tests risk-benefit of checkpoint inhibitors vs transplant loss, surgical management, and immunosuppression modification.

cases.cancer_type: Nodal marginal zone lymphoma of the neck (MALT lymphoma, cervical nodes)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Lymphoma of the neck — completely different from carcinoma. Tests strategy's ability to pivot to hematologic oncology protocols (rituximab, BR, watch-and-wait), Sjogren's-associated MALT lymphoma, and the role of treating underlying autoimmune disease.
